content: I extracted the report successfully and identified the biomarker values. I’m now writing a structured analysis (`JSON` + readable `MD`) into the execution folder, then I’ll mark `metadata.json` as completed and add one concise learning note to `MEMORY.md`.

/bin/zsh -lc "cat > executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/analysis.md << 'EOF'
# CBC Report Analysis

## Source
- File: "'`data/CBC-test-report-format-example-sample-template-Drlogy-lab-report.pdf`
- Execution ID: `14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77`

## Patient Context
- Age: 21 years
- Sex: Male
- PID: 555

## Key Biomarker Summary
| Marker | Result | Reference | Status |
|---|---:|---|---|
| Hemoglobin (Hb) | 12.5 g/dL | 13.0 - 17.0 | Low |
| Total RBC count | 5.2 mill/cumm | 4.5 - 5.5 | Normal |
| Total WBC count | 9000 cumm | 4000 - 11000 | Normal |
| Neutrophils | 60 % | 50 - 62 | Normal |
| Lymphocytes | 31 % | 20 - 40 | Normal |
| Eosinophils | 1 % | 00 - 06 | Normal |
| Monocytes | 7 % | 00 - 10 | Normal |
| Basophils | 1 % | 00 - 02 | Normal |
| Platelet Count | 150000 cumm | 150000 - 410000 | Borderline (lower limit) |
| Packed Cell Volume (PCV) | 57.5 % | 40 - 50 | High |
| Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV) | 87.75 fL | 83 - 101 | Normal |
| MCH | 27.2 pg | 27 - 32 | Normal |
| MCHC | 32.8 g/dL | 32.5 - 34.5 | Normal |
| RDW | 13.6 % | 11.6 - 14.0 | Normal |

## Abnormal / Highlighted Markers
- Hemoglobin (Hb): Low (12.5 g/dL)
- Packed Cell Volume (PCV): High (57.5%)
- Platelet count: Borderline at lower limit (150000)

## Basic Health Observations
- White cell count and differential distribution are within the report'"'s reference ranges.
- RBC indices (MCV, MCH, MCHC, RDW) are within normal limits.
- Mildly low hemoglobin with normal RBC indices can still warrant anemia workup depending on symptoms and clinical history.
- Elevated PCV may need correlation with hydration status and clinical context.
- Report includes lab note: \"Further confirm for Anemia\".

## Important Note
This interpretation is informational only and is not a medical diagnosis. Please review with a qualified clinician, especially if symptoms are present.
EOF"

content: I’ve written the structured analysis files. Next I’m finalizing execution metadata (`status`, timestamps, return code) and appending one high-value lesson to `MEMORY.md`.

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/metadata.json')
data = json.loads(p.read_text())
now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
started = datetime.fromisoformat(data['started_at'])
data['status'] = 'completed'
data['return_code'] = 0
data['completed_at'] = now.isoformat()
data['duration_seconds'] = (now - started).total_seconds()
p.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + '\\n')
PY"

content: I found one issue in `metadata.json`: timestamp timezone mismatch caused a negative duration. I’m correcting it to local naive time format consistent with `started_at`.

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - << 'PY'
import json
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/metadata.json')
data = json.loads(p.read_text())
now = datetime.now()
started = datetime.fromisoformat(data['started_at'])
data['completed_at'] = now.isoformat()
data['duration_seconds'] = round((now - started).total_seconds(), 3)
p.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + '\\n')
PY"

content:
Blood report analysis is completed for execution `14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77`.

Key findings from the CBC sample:
- Hemoglobin (Hb): `12.5 g/dL` (`Low`; ref `13.0 - 17.0`)
- Packed Cell Volume (PCV): `57.5%` (`High`; ref `40 - 50`)
- Platelet Count: `150000` (`Borderline`, at lower limit; ref `150000 - 410000`)
- RBC count, WBC count, differential counts, MCV/MCH/MCHC/RDW: within reported reference ranges

Interpretation summary:
- Pattern suggests possible mild anemia context (also aligns with report note: “Further confirm for Anemia”).
- Elevated PCV should be correlated clinically (for example hydration/other factors).
- Platelets are not low, but at the lower edge of normal; trend monitoring may be useful if symptomatic.

Saved outputs:
- [analysis.md](/Users/faizanpersonal/Desktop/Personal/Experimenting/agent/backend/workflow/executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/analysis.md)
- [analysis.json](/Users/faizanpersonal/Desktop/Personal/Experimenting/agent/backend/workflow/executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/analysis.json)
- [extracted_text.txt](/Users/faizanpersonal/Desktop/Personal/Experimenting/agent/backend/workflow/executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/extracted_text.txt)
- [metadata.json](/Users/faizanpersonal/Desktop/Personal/Experimenting/agent/backend/workflow/executions/blood_report_analyser/14fb29e5-d80d-4fcc-b394-bef04c50af77/metadata.json)

This is informational only and not a medical diagnosis; clinician review is recommended.
